Giving my two cent.
1. Use your proto horizon, on Enlightenments that matter for your main team. If it consists of Thais, it could be not bad, but we would need to know your other options on your main team.
2. You can run Thais in alot of teams as she functions as a accelerator or burst turn enabler. Most commonly, a Cantena team.
3. I would say E3 Castor is REALLY good as it allows him to become a damage dealing warden and can carry literally any team by himself. He can keep himself alive while killing them enemy at the same time. If you want to spread your power to build a E2 Tawil, then you could as well. Having more options for D-Tide is very good. GMurphy is a tad further and you should be fine as long as you have at least 60 pulls.
4. You should go for Horla as Nia stated. Solid Awakener at E0, due to her Joy stance which enables you to retain all cards in hand and stack your Aritha that is unused the turn before. At E1, she becomes a amazing support that applies Vulnerable on Strike, and Weakness on Defend. Wanda is kind…. meh. For Daffodil, E2 is strong, but Daff needs a strong team to make her work as a dps as she needs dmg amp from built supports. Last Verse or Treasured Rarity are good offensive WoDs, but Last Verse is prob more versatile.
5. You can use your proto paradise on three different options. One, to choose characters you wouldn’t pull on the current realm standard banner you choose(Aequor Banner, so you would proto Goliath). Two, to OverExalt a character. Three, use them to get a specific Enlightenment on your current team that significantly amps your team.
6. Use them on your main team, and yes, you should level everything to max. I would say do a little bit of thinking before jumping the gun. Make sure your main/best team can work well moving forward in story/D-tide/event, etc.
Those are my thoughts, consider your own, and others.

I would say build 3-4 teams that work well for D-Tide. Then focus on your main team for story-mode clears. For summons, ask around and see what characters are good and what works well together. Most importantly make sure you like the character for how they look or play. For standard pulls, get most characters, and then go to wheel jail. Pull for +12 Titan Blade, and then more WoDs.
Tawil seem really good for proccing corrosion stacks
Tawil is tiring to play and complex, but once you understand her, it comes down to how fast you can think and play.
If its not too much asked, do you have a recommendation of characters I should level for those 3/4 teams? Or if im missing a couple of chars II should get?
Simultaneously

Having a solid, strong, main team for story clear is perfectly fine. The reason I stress for 3-4 teams for D-Tide is because you want to push for 325(if I remember right) to get end game materials that allows your characters to reach level 70. You want to achieve this as soon as possible as it’s time gated(D-tide resets every 2 weeks). Clearing main story isn’t time gated for any endgame materials but covenants. Covenants are not as important as the Lvl Cap Limit release, imo.

Karen falls into Aritha Awakeners. Mainly a group of Awakeners that allow you to generate more Arithma which means more flexibility and cards can be played. There is Hameln, Celeste, Tinct, and one more(I think, I forgor maybe).
Your main team works perfectly fine.
You can’t really brick your account in any games. It’s just time gated, and nobody has patience, and everyone has FOMO
If you’re not enjoying the game you play, then it’s just work, not fun

I mean-
What kind of team do you want to build? It’s more of personal choice.

For example, your main team seems to be a “Stack Rats” comp, where you get Jenkin Rats infinitely and kill them via Rat spam.

For your other teams, what do you want to do. Stack counter? Stack strength? Stack poison? Infinite Devouring? Tentacle Slap Spam? One Shot Turn? Etc.

From their, build a team that plays around that.
Example is that Ogier would be in a Str stacking/Shield Comp. Caecus would be in a Counter Comp. Winkle would be in a Exalt spam comp. Nautila would be in a Counter/Shield Comp.
You seem to be missing a bunch of standard units, so I would start with getting more. Enough for 3-4 teams.
D-Tide, you need 5 teams, but the last team can be carried, via a max lvl or very high lvl support friend.

Flares is a amazing and smart Morimens player. The problem is he is a content creator, therefore he dramatizes alot and sometimes says wrong or stupid things for content. He knows it but its for content creation.
So if you are new or can’t understand and separate when he is lying for content versus the truth.
It becomes hard to learn or understand Flares unless you have a good understanding on the game already. Which means most new players fall into a “Flare’s Trap” in a way.
That’s not to say he is a bad Morimens player thou. He has cleared all content and constantly clears highest lvl of D-Tide.

Jenkin Clem Castor Erica:
Jenkin generates rats. Clem gives you survival with her heals, and skey for Posse, and Exalt can increase burst turn dmg or decrease enemies burst turn. Castor can offer dmg multiplier via Corrosion, and gives massive shields, with card draw and free dmg, and weakness application on 2 cost. Erica generates more skey for Posse with her 0 cost and her X cost is just a free card. Her Exalt can give you shield and apply weakness for enemy burst turn, and apply vulnerable for your burst turn, acting like a weaker Clem with similar usage.
Actually, Im gonna have you play Aequor Tawil rather then Chaos Tawil since she is more manageable in Aequor. Chaos requires more investment.
Chaos wants oe tawil really badly due to the horrible options in there
Tawil Hameln Myriam Ogier:
Posse is your own ability and skey is the awakener ability right?
Skey is the rousource used by Posse
resource

Tawil is a complex awakener, but ever so broken. She will discover cards from ANY awakener she is in the realm of. Her 2 cost gives you something that is a 3 proc card with various effects that are very powerful. With Tawil, you want card draw, and keyflare. Hameln enables Tawil excellently. Giving card draw, Arithma, a Exalt that double plays, and Keyflare, and a rouse that allows you to double play or -2 cost on your next card. Myriam can generate further tentacles and generate Aliemus to fuel more Exalts. Her rouse is a three proc card with powerful effects with a prepare ability that is -3 with every turn retained. Lastly, Ogier generates Strength, but more importantly gives you a big shield on Exalt and X cost card, giving you the much need survivability on the team.
You generate Skey to Posse. You generate Aliemus to Exalt. Posse is bottom left. Exalt is awakeners “ultimates”.
kat isnt the play sanga over miriam, ryker over oggie to well get to pinion faster
ryker also adds vuln+weak with dice

Daffodil Thais Aigis Winkle:
Hard team to manage. Daffodil wants to generate Exalts to stack her 4 cost big burst card into Ultra Turn every turn if possible, so when you enter ultra turn, it’s a nuke turn. Her 3 cost stacks a 25% dmg up with every enemy killed with it, making her overall kit very “bursty”. Thais has a 2 cost that generates Aliemus to further Exalts. Another 2 cost that allows you to generate Embyro for devour effects, and gives skey, and allows you to apply Weak or Vuln. Her Exalt allows duplication of a card, allowing a even bigger burst turn. Aigis helps generate further skey with her 3 cost “free” card as long as the enemy has Vuln, and her crucial 1 turn stun Exalt which can be the decider of many fights. Winkle is your defensive with weak application and even more skey, and shields. Her exalt furthers other awakeners Exalts meaning, you know it more burst, faster burst. This team is all about Posse and bursting as fast as possible.
My only worry is Sanga shields are expensive, and Ogier is more flexible and cheaper. Ryker works well but I’m worried about survivability.

Ramona Nautila Sanga Ryker:
With this team, focus is on Sanga Exalt, burst turns with Nautila Exalt as well. Ramona serves as Skey and Str down for enemies, and the powerful Exalt of searching for cards and making them 0 cost. The shuffle 1 Insight is very nice sometimes. Nautila will build high defensive shields for Sanga burst while her counter can help with mobbing. Her Exalt increases current shield by 50% so you want to stack em up and then Exalt. Then Sanga enters with fragile removal next turn with some planning, and her Exalt Dmg scales off of your shield. Like Nia said, build a defensive WoDs and Convenants to maximize shield size as that equals Sanga Exalt dmg. Lastly Ryker, doesnt exactly fit but eh. He can search cards and allow further card draw with his 1 cost and Exalt. He can also give you special relics that give you keyflare and a dice on Posse. The dice rolls a RNG 6 dice that if you land 4 or higher, you apply weakness and Vuln. This is the weird team, but with a bit of wackness, can work.

For WoDs, read the fine print on the talents and passives and skills. See if they get benefits from having a specific wheel. Then read if the benefit is worth it or necessary.
A specific stat, not wheel. My bad
Most dps will run crit/crit dmg. Supports mostly run aliemus or silver key gen.
But that’s only a general rule
Read the passives and talents scalings.
should I roll for Castor's WoD or Tawil E1 first
Furthermore, duplicate wheels should be “fused” since you can only run one of each wheel on each team.

Additionally, for storymode, use the brown/r wheels on supports called “Frenzy” and “Gluttony” as they are broken. “Frenzy” allows buying cursed relics which are overpowered. “Gluttony allows forgoing something(I forgot) for increase in maximum hp. These are very strong until Arc2(After Chapter 9 or D-Tide).
You can get either, up to you

Since her E2 is her best breaking point.
